summary: Martin Fowler shares his six favorite musical discoveries from 2025, continuing an annual tradition. He describes his listening habits—buying digital albums in batches rather than using streaming services. The selections span Balkan brass, acoustic pop, Ethio-Jazz, Latin acoustic, Indian classical fusion, and European jazz-prog, reflecting eclectic world music tastes. Each pick is accompanied by personal context about how he discovered the artist and what makes the album distinctive.
key_points: Fowler deliberately avoids streaming services, preferring to buy and deeply listen to digital albums in rotating batches
His 2025 picks span a remarkably wide geographic and stylistic range: Balkan, Ethiopian, Latin American, Indian, and European jazz traditions
Several selections involve reunion or late-career albums from long-followed artists like Fairground Attraction and Shakti
He credits specific music journalism sources—Songlines magazine, Bandcamp columns, and the OK Jazz podcast—for discovery
money_quotes: I don't use streaming services, every couple of weeks I buy a bunch of digital albums and listen to them every day until we're ready for a new batch.
It's impossible for this reunion album to quite match one of my favorite albums of all time, but after all these years they are still a remarkable band.

This album takes several songs from her earlier albums and rearranges them in a simple acoustic format - which reveals how strong they are, and how well she can fill an album with just her voice and guitar playing.
His music is often wordless, sometimes with invented words, chosen for their sound rather than any meaning.
Mind Explosion is a live album taken from their 50th anniversary tour done just before Zakir Hussain's death.
key_insight: This is a personal music recommendation post with no software engineering content—Fowler shares his deliberate, non-streaming approach to music discovery and his eclectic world music tastes.
